# String functions: uppercase, lowercase, replace

Verified string transforms in `alter`: `uppercase(x)`, `lowercase(x)`, and `replace(<field>, "<old>", "<new>")` (literal substring replace). Use `lowercase()` to normalize case before grouping or matching (e.g. `Powershell.EXE` vs `powershell.exe` collapse to one). Pairs with the verified extractors from the arrays/regex set: `split`, `arrayindex`, `regextract`, `json_extract_scalar`.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_image_name != null
| alter proc_norm = lowercase(actor_process_image_name)
| comp count() as execs by proc_norm
| sort desc execs
```

_Stage/function syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (Advanced-auth PAPI, narrow window). The mechanics are confirmed; adapt field names to your dataset._
